textPlain: This article is written at this stage of every cycle. It’s getting old.
Sinking in value. Expanding nearly exponentially in breadth and depth of financial system integration.
Really, it can’t sink to zero fast enough. It’s sole value proposition is a means to facilitate criminal activity.
In a few weeks it will pump again and people will write that crypto is the future.
Does it do anything useful yet?
>It’s sole value proposition is a means to facilitate criminal activityI used to feel this way until visa/master pulled that project-2025-mandated stunt on nsfw games.
To be honest, that probably means the value isn’t zero.
Most crime is conducted in fiat money.

Meanwhile you have the credit card processors, and traditional banks.These will often refuse or vastly over charge perfectly legal business that they have a "moral" objection to. This is by design.Dammed if you do, and dammed if you dont.
